methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
EX-Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Kazuki Matsuo and Shinsuke Fujioka that has raised $37M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private-stage nuclear energy firms including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation lever here. Using the $37M in total capital raised as a proxy for pre-money valuation, a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier applied to Series A progression yields an implied post-money valuation of $74M. Peer-median triangulation was not executable given insufficient granularity in the nuclear energy comp set; while 8 private peers exist in the sector, filtering by stage and valuation tier did not yield the three or more datapoints needed for reliable median benchmarking. Applying an all-sector median would risk material distortion given the narrow and capital-intensive nature of frontier nuclear development.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $63M (low) to $85M (high), centered on $74M. This single-method output carries meaningful uncertainty: the absence of stage-matched peer multiples leaves the 2.0x assumption unvalidated against comparable financings. Key sensitivities include technology readiness, regulatory pathway clarity, and the pace of capital deployment in the Series A-to-B window.
